info.title: 🚀 Starbase API Documentation
info.description:
## Overview
The **BisectHosting Server API** provides powerful, off-panel server management and data tools. This API allows you to automate server tasks, retrieve essential data, and integrate your applications with BisectHosting's services.

All API routes are **authenticated** and require a valid API key. You can generate an API key in the BisectHosting Panel under the **Account API Keys** section.

## Getting Started
### Authentication
All API requests require an API key to be included in the request headers. Use the following format:

```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

Most requests also require a **server UUID** in the URL path. You can find this in your server information panel.

### Base URL
All API requests should be directed to:

```
https://games.bisecthosting.com/api/client/{route}
```

## Rate Limits
To ensure fair usage and prevent abuse, API requests are subject to rate limits. The specific rate limits are:

- **Requests per minute**: `720`

Exceeding these limits will result in a `429 Too Many Requests` response. We recommend implementing exponential backoff in your requests.

## Error Handling
Our API returns standard HTTP status codes to indicate the success or failure of a request.

| Status Code | Meaning |
|-------------|---------|
| **200 OK** | Request was successful. |
| **201 Created** | Resource was successfully created. |
| **400 Bad Request** | The request was malformed or contained invalid parameters. |
| **401 Unauthorized** | Missing or invalid API key. |
| **403 Forbidden** | You do not have permission to access this resource. |
| **404 Not Found** | The requested resource was not found. |
| **429 Too Many Requests** | Rate limit exceeded. |
| **500 Internal Server Error** | A server-side error occurred. |

## Common Headers
| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `Authorization` | Bearer token for authentication. |
| `Content-Type` | Must be `application/json` for POST and PUT requests. |
| `Accept` | Determines the response format. Commonly: `application/json`. |

## Example Request
Here's an example of a request using `curl`:

```sh
curl -X GET "https://games.bisecthosting.com/api/client/servers/{server_uuid}" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
     -H "Accept: application/json"
```

## WebSocket Connection Process
1. **Get Token**: Call the [WebSocket endpoint](#tag/Server-JWT) to retrieve the JWT token and WebSocket URL
2. **Establish Connection**: Connect to the WebSocket URL using a WebSocket client
3. **Authenticate**: Send an authentication message with the JWT token
4. **Receive Events**: Listen for server events and status updates
5. **Send Commands**: Send console commands and control server state

  ### Authentication Flow

  After connecting to the WebSocket, you must authenticate by sending:
  ```json
  {
    "event": "auth",
    "args": ["YOUR_JWT_TOKEN_HERE"]
  }
  ```

  Upon successful authentication, you'll receive:
  ```json
  {
    "event": "auth success"
  }
  ```

  ### Available Events to Send

  | Event | Description | Required Permission |
  |-------|-------------|-------------------|
  | `auth` | Authenticate the connection | `websocket.connect` |
  | `send command` | Execute console command | `control.console` |
  | `set state` | Change server power state | `control.start`, `control.stop`, or `control.restart` |
  | `send logs` | Request recent console logs | - |
  | `send stats` | Request server resource stats | - |
  | `send player list` | Request current player list | - |
  | `send player chat` | Request recent chat messages | - |
  | `send live activity update` | Request live activity updates | - |

  ### Events You'll Receive

  | Event | Description | Data Format |
  |-------|-------------|-------------|
  | `console output` | Server console output | `["log line"]` |
  | `status` | Server status change | `["starting"] | ["running"]` | ["stopping"] | `["offline"]` |
  | `stats` | Resource usage stats | `["{"memory":{"current":1024},...}"]` |
  | `install output` | Installation progress | `["install log line"]` |
  | `backup completed` | Backup completion | `["backup_id"]` |
  | `player list` | Current players | `["[{"name":"Player1",...}]"]` |
  | `player chat` | Chat messages | `["{"player":"Name","message":"Hello"}"]` |
  | `live activity` | Live activity updates | `["{"action":"file.upload",...}"]` |
  | `daemon error` | Error messages | `["error description"]` |
  | `jwt error` | Authentication errors | `["error description"]` |
  | `token expiring` | Token expiration warning | `[]` |
  | `token expired` | Token has expired | `[]` |

  ### Power State Control

  To control server power state, send:
  ```json
  {
    "event": "set state",
    "args": ["start|stop|restart|kill"]
  }
  ```

  ### Sending Console Commands

  To execute console commands, send:
  ```json
  {
    "event": "send command",
    "args": ["your console command here"]
  }
  ```

  ### Token Management

  - Tokens expire after 10 minutes
  - You'll receive `token expiring` event 3 minutes before expiry
  - You'll receive `token expired` event when token expires
  - Call this endpoint again to get a new token and re-authenticate

  ### Example JavaScript Implementation

  ```javascript
  // Get WebSocket details
  const response = await fetch('https://games.bisecthosting.com/api/client/servers/{server}/websocket', {
    headers: { 'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY' }
  });
  const { data } = await response.json();

  // Connect to WebSocket
  const ws = new WebSocket(data.socket);

  ws.onopen = () => {
    // Authenticate
    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
      event: 'auth',
      args: [data.token]
    }));
  };

  ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    const { event: eventName, args } = JSON.parse(event.data);

    switch(eventName) {
      case 'auth success':
        console.log('WebSocket authenticated successfully');
        // Request initial server status
        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ event: 'send stats' }));
        break;
      case 'console output':
        console.log('Console:', args[0]);
        break;
      case 'status':
        console.log('Server status:', args[0]);
        break;
      case 'stats':
        console.log('Server stats:', JSON.parse(args[0]));
        break;
      case 'token expiring':
        // Refresh token before it expires
        refreshWebSocketToken();
        break;
    }
  };
  ```

  ### Connection URL Format

  The WebSocket URL is returned from the [WebSocket endpoint](#tag/Server-JWT)

## Need Help?
If you encounter any issues or have questions about using the API, feel free to reach out:

- 📖 **Documentation**: [API Docs Link](https://games.bisecthosting.com/docs)
- 💬 **Community Support**: [BisectHosting Discord](https://discord.gg/bisecthosting)
- 📨 **Support Ticket**: [BisectHosting Support](https://bisecthosting.com/support)

---
This document is subject to updates and improvements as the API evolves.

post.description:
Performs power actions (start, stop, restart, kill) on multiple servers simultaneously.
Only servers that the user has permission to control will be affected.

requestBody.description:
Performs power actions (start, stop, restart, kill) on multiple servers simultaneously.
Only servers that the user has permission to control will be affected.

servers.description: Comma-separated list of server IDs to perform the action on.

action.description: The power action to perform. Must be one of: start, stop, restart, kill.

example.message: Bulk power action queued successfully.

example.error: You do not have permission to perform this action on any of the requested servers.

curl -X POST "https://games.bisecthosting.com/api/client/bulk/power" 
    -d "servers"="1,2,3,4" \
    -d "action"="restart"
